channel.title: Home on mazo | cybersec
channel.description: Recent content in Home on mazo | cybersec

item.title: AI vs CTF
item.description:
Intro

First off, I just want to say thanks to anyone who actually took the time to click on this and read it. It honestly means a lot.

One more thing before I start. These are just my personal opinions and thoughts. You don’t have to agree with me and that’s completely normal. Everyone is different and that’s how it should be.

How It Started vs. How It Feels Now

I’ve been playing CTFs for a few years now. I’ve done a lot of different competitions and I always enjoyed them. But this year something changed. I started asking myself if it’s still worth it. Like, does it still give me the same feeling of joy and fulfillment that it used to?

item.title: Challenge / Lab Name
item.description:
overview

Short description of the challenge or lab.

  • type: web / linux / network / misc
  • source: CTF / lab / own setup
  • goal: what needed to be achieved

initial analysis

[ observations ]

What you noticed first.

  • open ports / services
  • files / endpoints
  • anything unusual

enumeration

[ tools ]

  • nmap
  • gobuster

[ commands ]

nmap -sC -sV <target>
gobuster dir -u http://<target> -w <wordlist>

item.title: achievements
item.description:
CyberGame Slovakia 2026

Player certificate from CyberGame Slovakia 2026. Placed 17th with 75 / 83 solved tasks and 22165 / 26046 points.
